target_audience: Beginner developers and operators who are just starting to work with Kubernetes clusters (assumed to have a basic understanding of containers and Docker)

lectures.title: kubectl CLI Basics: Core Commands and Cluster Operations
objectives: Understand the kubeconfig structure and how to switch between contexts and namespaces, enabling safe navigation across multiple cluster environments
Use core inspection and debugging commands — get, describe, logs, and exec — along with output format and filtering options such as -o wide/yaml/json and --selector to assess resource state
Distinguish between the imperative style (create/run/apply -f) and the declarative style (apply), and choose the right resource management approach for each situation
